title: Thunderstorms, rain, heatwave conditions to continue: APSDMA - The Hans India
full_text:
Thunderstorms, rain, heatwave conditions to continue: APSDMA
Vijayawada: Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) managing director Prakhar Jain said that the state will continue to experience varied weather conditions over the next three days, with thunderstorms and rainfall expected in several districts while heatwave conditions persist in others.
According to weather forecasts, on Saturday, light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning is likely at isolated places in Polavaram, East Godavari, Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Palnadu, Markapuram, Kurnool and Nandyal districts. Light rain with thunderstorms is also expected in parts of the remaining districts.
Jain advised people not to take shelter under trees, electric poles, or hoardings during thunderstorms. Farmers and cattle rearers have been urged to remain vigilant and move to safer locations immediately if dark clouds and thunder are observed. He also warned people to stay away from fallen or damaged electricity lines during strong winds and rain.
The APSDMA has forecast severe heatwave conditions in 56 mandals and heatwave conditions in 51 mandals on Saturday. The affected areas include 11 mandals in Srikakulam district, 20 in Vizianagaram, 14 in Parvathipuram Manyam, one in Alluri Sitharama Raju district, two in Polavaram region and eight in Kakinada district.
Temperatures between 42°C and 44°C are likely in parts of Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Parvathipuram Manyam, Alluri Sitharama Raju, Polavaram and Kakinada districts. Temperatures ranging from 40°C to 42°C may be recorded in Anakapalli, Konaseema, East and West Godavari, Eluru, Krishna, NTR, Guntur, Bapatla and Palnadu districts. On Friday, the highest temperature of 44.6°C was recorded at Gudur in Nellore district, followed by 43.7°C at Tanuku, 43.3°C at Piduguralla, and 43.1°C at Kanumolu and Salur.
The Disaster Management Authority advised the public, particularly senior citizens, pregnant women and nursing mothers, to take adequate precautions against extreme heat. People have been urged to avoid unnecessary outdoor exposure during peak daytime hours and stay hydrated by consuming lemon water, buttermilk, coconut water and sufficient drinking water. Those suffering from heart ailments, diabetes and blood pressure-related conditions have been advised to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun.
